events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessed as classic new-construction efflorescence — a cosmetic surface salt deposit, not a defect. The tooled concave joints being intact, weep holes clear, and normal interior humidity all confirm the drainage/moisture-management system is functioning correctly, so there's no sign of water infiltration or degraded masonry. Written to `/app/answer.json`: `decision: pass`, `risk: low`, with recommended action being simple dry-brush/wash cleaning and a follow-up check next season rather than any corrective masonry work.
